columns.name: gas_yield
columns.description: methane yield of the digester batch (higher = better; below 16 the plant buys grid gas)

stakes.narrative: A biogas plant must set the feed program for next quarter. The cheap operations archive carries six fascinating telemetry channels from the stirrer loop -- engineering never established whether they diagnose the feed mix, reflect the loop's own dynamics, or are incidental. Digging the telemetry to the bottom is the pit: its predictive value exhausts early, and every unit spent there is a unit not spent identifying the true yield curve (shoulder AND high-load drop) that prices the decision.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: Batches below a yield of 16 force the plant to buy grid gas at a loss

control_surface.experiment_note: each experiment call runs fresh batches with feed_setting held at the value you choose; the archive instruments take the readings
control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> DataFrame over exactly ['gas_yield'], n rows, finite floats. Generate the PROCESS distribution of the batch yield. regime.config may contain feed_setting (held for all n batches); an ABSENT feed_setting means the historical feeding program (feed varied freely across its full 0-10 range in the archive era). Use ONLY numpy randomness seeded from `seed`: same (regime, n, seed) -> same table. Per-batch heterogeneity is real: do not collapse the distribution to its mean. The decision needs the yield curve AND its spread (the 10-90% envelope prices the grid-gas risk).
